design_philosophy.core_loop: Select one of seven characters, quest across world to collect seven magical runes while competing against other playable characters; avoid or engage randomly-spawning enemies using crystal ball radar; progress through towns and dungeons with branching party compositions
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (half gold lost on defeat) combined with competitive multiplayer narrative where other playable characters are both allies and antagonists; the unknown traitor mechanic creates strategic uncertainty
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Being one of seven chosen apprentices competing for power and kingship; building alliances with rival heroes while ultimately seeking ultimate inheritance
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game commits to punishing localization balance decisions (reduced player stat growth vs. other characters who retain original scaling); removes randomness from encounter initiation through radar system but keeps enemies aggressive and plentiful; narrative twist reframes the entire quest as manipulation by the hidden final antagonist

difficulty.continues_system: Defeat results in loss of half current gold; losing to other playable characters results in loss of runes
difficulty.checkpointing: Towns function as safe zones for rest and item management; no mid-dungeon checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can theoretically avoid encounters using crystal ball radar, but enemies move quickly and are numerous, making avoidance extremely difficult; game is intentionally unforgiving due to North American localization stat rebalancing where player character receives reduced stat increases compared to AI rivals

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon and armor purchases from shops; spell learning and stat growth through leveling; character selection at start determines unique items and spells available throughout playthrough
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression locked behind collection of seven runes scattered across world; access to runes controlled by powerful tyrants (wizards, kings, dragons) who must be defeated; bounty hunter Pison hunts player based on randomized traitor identity
replayability_hooks: Seven distinct playable characters with unique stat progressions and spell lists
Randomized traitor identity changes bounty hunter pursuit patterns per playthrough
Multiple alliance/duel options with other playable characters affect party composition
Character interactions and story context vary based on chosen protagonist

world_building_techniques: NPC dialogue in towns reveals lore about Saro, Gorsia, and five-thousand-year cycle
Environmental storytelling through ancient ruins of Melenam revealing future technological origin
Narrative framing devices: player dispatched by King Lemele, later revealed as Gorsia in disguise
Time-travel revelation that recontextualizes entire first half of game
Character-specific story branches through alliance/duel system with rival apprentices

boss_design.philosophy: Powerful tyrants serve as rune guardians; final boss is narrative twist where player discovers true antagonist has been orchestrating entire quest; boss fights serve as story checkpoints and resource challenges
notable_bosses: Gariso (defeated early, later resurrected as story checkpoint)
Lemele/Gorsia (final boss; revealed as 5000-year-old antagonist who time-traveled and assumed false identity)
Super-Tetsujin (rogue robot encountered in past timeline; represents consequence of hubris)

faction_indicators: Seven apprentices form implicit factions through alliance/opposition mechanics
Bounty hunter Pison represents enforcement of unseen authority
Character choice at start signals ideological/fantasy preference (human, elf, dwarf, robots, demon, alien)
Randomized traitor creates distrust among peer group
danger_signals: Gorsia's 5000-year manipulation of world history reveals systemic deception at institutional level
King Lemele's gentle authority conceals hostile takeover and resource extraction scheme
Bounty hunter acts as enforcer for hidden power structure
Rune collection quest frames competitive cooperation as covert power consolidation
environmental_storytelling: Ancient ruins of Melenam recontextualized as future technological city destroyed by its own creation
Physical world changes between 5000-year-past and present timelines showing civilization cycles
Tyrant-controlled territories throughout world show fragmented power distribution
Towns serve as safe spaces highlighting contrast with hostile wilderness
